description.p: You hurl a bolt of crimson flame shaped like a bat at a creature you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 fire damage.

Two embers spiral away from the target. Each creature of your choice within 5 feet of the target must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 1d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

You summon a cloak of swirling nightmare flames that surrounds you for the spell's duration. Until the spell ends, opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage, and whenever a creature hits you with an attack while within 5 feet of you, that creature takes 1d6 fire damage.

You perform a ritual dance of crimson flame, teleporting between three creatures within 30 feet of you. For each creature you target, make a melee spell attack. On a hit, that creature takes 3d8 fire damage. You can move up to 15 feet between each attack.
At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 3rd.

You summon visions of a nightmare parade that horrifies creatures around you. Each creature within 20 feet of you that can see you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes 3d8 psychic damage and is frightened of you until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and isn't frightened.

A crimson lantern manifests and hovers above you for the spell's duration. While the lantern is active, when you cast a spell that deals fire damage, one damage roll of that spell gains a bonus equal to the number of creatures within 10 feet of you that you can see, up to a maximum bonus of 1d8.
Additionally, creatures have disadvantage on saving throws against fear that are caused by spells or abilities of the Nightmare Heart Warlock.
